root_text.iti38:2.1: “Tathāgataṁ, bhikkhave, arahantaṁ sammāsambuddhaṁ dve vitakkā bahulaṁ samudācaranti—
root_text.iti38:2.4: Tamenaṁ, bhikkhave, tathāgataṁ abyāpajjhārāmaṁ abyāpajjharataṁ eseva vitakko bahulaṁ samudācarati:
root_text.iti38:2.5: ‘imāyāhaṁ iriyāya na kañci byābādhemi tasaṁ vā thāvaraṁ vā’ti.
root_text.iti38:3.2: Tamenaṁ, bhikkhave, tathāgataṁ pavivekārāmaṁ pavivekarataṁ eseva vitakko bahulaṁ samudācarati:
root_text.iti38:4.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, tumhepi abyāpajjhārāmā viharatha abyāpajjharatā.
root_text.iti38:4.2: Tesaṁ vo, bhikkhave, tumhākaṁ abyāpajjhārāmānaṁ viharataṁ abyāpajjharatānaṁ eseva vitakko bahulaṁ samudācarissati:
root_text.iti38:4.3: ‘imāya mayaṁ iriyāya na kañci byābādhema tasaṁ vā thāvaraṁ vā’ti.
root_text.iti38:5.2: Tesaṁ vo, bhikkhave, tumhākaṁ pavivekārāmānaṁ viharataṁ pavivekaratānaṁ eseva vitakko bahulaṁ samudācarissati:
root_text.iti38:9.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti38:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti38:2.1: “Two thoughts, mendicants, often occur to the Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha:
translation_text.iti38:2.2: the thought of sanctuary, and that of seclusion.
translation_text.iti38:2.3: The Realized One loves kindness and delights in it,
translation_text.iti38:2.4: so this thought often occurs to him:
translation_text.iti38:2.5: ‘Through this behavior, I shall not hurt any creature firm or frail.’
translation_text.iti38:3.1: The Realized One loves seclusion and delights in it,
translation_text.iti38:3.2: so this thought often occurs to him:
translation_text.iti38:3.3: ‘What is unskillful has been given up.’
translation_text.iti38:4.1: So, mendicants, you too should love kindness and delight in it,
translation_text.iti38:4.2: then this thought will often occur to you:
translation_text.iti38:4.3: ‘Through this behavior, I shall not hurt any creature firm or frail.’
translation_text.iti38:5.1: You too should love seclusion and delight in it,
translation_text.iti38:5.2: then this thought will often occur to you:
translation_text.iti38:5.3: ‘What is unskillful? What is not given up? What should I give up?’”
translation_text.iti38:6.2: the Realized One, the bearer of the unbearable:
translation_text.iti38:6.3: first mentioned was thought of sanctuary,
translation_text.iti38:6.4: then the second made clear was seclusion.
translation_text.iti38:7.1: Dispeller of darkness, the great seer has crossed over:
translation_text.iti38:7.2: the attained, the master, the undefiled.
translation_text.iti38:7.3: In the midst of it all, <j>he is freed in the ending of craving;
translation_text.iti38:7.4: that sage bears his final body.
translation_text.iti38:7.5: He has disposed of Māra, I declare, <j>and gone beyond old age.
translation_text.iti38:8.1: Standing high on a rocky mountain,
translation_text.iti38:8.2: you can see the people all around.
translation_text.iti38:8.3: In just the same way, the all-seer, so intelligent,
translation_text.iti38:8.4: having ascended the Temple of Truth,
translation_text.iti38:8.5: rid of sorrow, looks upon the people
translation_text.iti38:8.6: swamped with sorrow, overcome by rebirth and old age.”
translation_text.iti38:9.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
